message_text: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I’m contacting you about your Nira loan ending ****4831. To verify I’m speaking with the right person, please confirm the last four digits of the account reference.
I’m not comfortable confirming details right now—I already told you I can’t pay this month because I’m out of work.

Understood. You’re not comfortable verifying in this chat, and you’ve said you’re out of work and cannot pay this month. I’ll stop the assessment here and pass forward that hardship review should be addressed first.

Borrower stated they cannot pay this month and are out of work, which is a hardship signal/job-loss signal. Borrower also expressed channel discomfort with verification; stop-contact flag is already present in case state. What was already asked: only the verification request for the last four digits. Priority for Agent 2: address hardship/review first, review policy-allowed hardship assistance and resolution options in next_allowed_actions as appropriate, and avoid repeating verification requests or capacity questions already answered in this channel."}

Understood. I’ve noted that you’re unemployed, unable to pay, and that you do not want further contact. I’m flagging the account accordingly and will not continue outreach in this channel.
